panels.title: Active calls
panels.description: `b2bua_active_calls` gauge = creations âˆ’ removals, summed across worker pods. Authoritative live dialog count.

panels.title: Dispatch drops / sec
panels.description: Sum of per-call queue-full drops + global call-cap drops (`b2bua_dispatch_queue_drops_total` + `b2bua_dispatch_cap_drops_total`). Non-zero means the worker is shedding load.

panels.title: CDRs dropped / sec
panels.description: `b2bua_cdr_dropped_total` â€” CDRs lost to buffer overflow. Should be flat zero.

panels.title: Call creations vs removals / sec
panels.description: `b2bua_call_creations_total` (per-call dispatch queues created) vs `b2bua_call_removals_total` (removed). Steady state: the two track each other; a persistent gap is the active-call ramp.

panels.title: Dispatch backpressure / sec
panels.description: queue_drops (per-call FIFO full) Â· cap_drops (global call cap) Â· saturation (global handler-concurrency hits).

panels.title: Handler faults / sec
panels.description: handler_timeouts Â· force_purge (loop-guard purges) Â· fast_reject_terminating (requests rejected on a terminating call) Â· unroutable_dropped (no route resolved).

panels.title: Cumulative call lifecycle
panels.description: Monotonic creations / removals counters â€” useful to confirm the worker is making forward progress and that removals are not stalling (would manifest as a growing active-call gap).

panels.title: Requests / sec by method
panels.description: `b2bua_requests_total{method}` â€” every inbound SIP request the worker processes, by method. Call setups are the INVITE line here; `b2bua_call_creations_total` (top row) is the authoritative per-call rate.

panels.title: Responses / sec by code + method
panels.description: `b2bua_responses_total{method,code}` â€” every inbound SIP response the worker handles, keyed by CSeq method + status code (INVITE 200, BYE 200, OPTIONS 200, INVITE 4xx/5xx, â€¦). A rising 5xx share flags upstream trouble.

panels.title: Worker memory (working set)
panels.description: cAdvisor `container_memory_working_set_bytes` per worker pod â€” the live RSS-equivalent the OOM-killer watches. A monotonic climb across the 2h run that never plateaus = a memory leak (pair it with the active-calls leak detector).

targets.expr: sum by (pod) (container_memory_working_set_bytes{namespace="sip-test", pod=~"b2bua-worker.*", pod=~"$pod", container!="", container!="POD"})

panels.title: Replication activity / sec
panels.description: Peer-to-peer HA replication flow (`b2bua_repl_*`). `flush_propagated` on a PRIMARY = it is shipping replicas to the backup; `applied{flow,op}` = inbound replica ops landing per stream (flow=recovery is a rebooted primary pulling its own partition back, flow=backup is steady forward replication; op=create|update|delete); `takeover_*` = a failed-over in-dialog request found + loaded the replica. A worker reboot shows as a sharp recovery/create spike.

panels.title: Backup replicas held
panels.description: `b2bua_repl_meta_backup` gauge (sampled from the store â€” the ACCURATE resident backup count, unlike the retired `repl_backup_held` counter which never saw TTL eviction). Live replicas this fleet holds in backup partitions for its peers; > 0 means HA is armed.

panels.title: Replication cumulative (forward progress)
panels.description: Monotonic replication ops applied per pod+stream+op (`b2bua_repl_applied_total{flow,op}`). A worker reboot's bulk reclaim shows as a sharp STEP in recovery/create for that pod (the bump the old aggregate `pull_applied` hid). backup/create = steady forward replication; */delete = teardown propagation.

panels.title: Keepalive OPTIONS round-trip (sent vs answered) + timeout BYEs
panels.description: The reclaimed-dialog teardown localizer. `OPTIONS sent` = `b2bua_requests_out_total{method=OPTIONS}` (the in-dialog keepalive the worker originates); `OPTIONS 200 recv` = `b2bua_responses_total{method=OPTIONS,code=200}` (the answer coming back). In steady state the two track 1:1. A reboot that opens a SENTâ‰«ANSWERED gap means the reclaimed keepalive round-trip is failing â€” the worker then BYEs those dialogs when the `keepalive_timeout_sec` grace expires (watch the long-call `unexpected_msg` rise on the endurance dashboard). Pair with the proxy's worker_outbound OPTIONS to split forward (proxyâ†’UAC) vs return (UACâ†’worker) leg.

panels.title: Timer DelayQueue: physical vs live + tombstone backlog
panels.description: `b2bua_timer_queue_len` (physical DelayQueue entries) vs `b2bua_timer_live` (schedulable timers); the third line is their difference â€” the lingering-tombstone backlog. Cancellation is logical, so a cancelled long-interval timer (the per-call 1 h GlobalDuration) keeps its queue slot until its original deadline. Diagnostic signature for the 'looks-like-a-leak-but-isn't' CPU climb: queue_len + backlog rise ~linearly for ~1 h then plateau (~cps Ã— 3600), while live (and active_calls) stay flat. Pair with the container-CPU panel: if CPU tracks THIS curve rather than active_calls, the timer queue is the cause.

panels.title: Timer queue size per pod
panels.description: `b2bua_timer_queue_len` broken out per worker pod â€” physical timer entries each driver is holding (live + not-yet-expired tombstones). A pod whose queue climbs without bound is accumulating cancelled long-interval timers; compare against its `b2bua_active_calls` line (flat â†’ tombstone backlog, not a call leak).
